title: Over Bleeding During Periods: Causes, Symptoms, and Medicinal Herbal Remedies

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus, also known as chasteberry, is a plant-based remedy that has been used for centuries to help with menstrual issues, including heavy bleeding. The herb is believed to work by regulating hormones in the body, which can help to reduce the amount of bleeding during periods. It is thought to affect the production of progesterone, a hormone that helps to thicken the uterine lining and prevent excessive bleeding. When progesterone levels are balanced, the uterine lining sheds less, resulting in lighter periods. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus may help to calm the uterus and reduce inflammation, further reducing bleeding. Some women also find it helps to ease other symptoms of PMS, such as mood swings and breast tenderness, which can be related to heavy bleeding.

Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, is a plant that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to help with menstrual problems. During periods, some women experience over bleeding, also known as menorrhagia, which can be painful and uncomfortable. Angelica sinensis is good for over bleeding because it helps to strengthen the uterus and balance the body's energy. The plant contains a compound called ferulic acid, which has been shown to reduce bleeding by shrinking the size of blood vessels. This helps to slow down the flow of blood during periods, making it a natural remedy for over bleeding. Additionally, Angelica sinensis has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce cramps and other symptoms associated with heavy bleeding. By taking Angelica sinensis in the form of tea, capsules, or tablets, women may be able to reduce the severity of their periods and improve their overall health.

Cimicifuga racemosa, also known as black cohosh, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with menstrual issues, including heavy bleeding. The roots of the plant contain compounds that can help to relax the uterus and reduce inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for women who experience heavy periods. Black cohosh has also been shown to help regulate menstrual cycles, which can help to reduce the frequency and severity of heavy bleeding. Additionally, it is believed to help reduce the formation of blood clots, which can contribute to heavy bleeding. When taken as a supplement, black cohosh can help to reduce the symptoms of menopause, including hot flashes and night sweats, which can also contribute to heavy bleeding. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for generations, and modern studies have supported its effectiveness in reducing menstrual bleeding. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using black cohosh, especially if you're already taking other medications or have any underlying health conditions.

Paeonia lactiflora, also known as Chinese peony, is a herb that has been used for centuries to help with heavy bleeding during periods. The roots of this plant contain compounds that have a calming effect on the uterus, which can help to slow down bleeding. Paeonia lactiflora is believed to help reduce inflammation and ease cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for menstrual issues. The plant is thought to work by regulating the body's hormonal balance, which can help to normalize menstrual flow. Studies have shown that Paeonia lactiflora can reduce the severity of heavy bleeding and improve symptoms of anemia. Additionally, it is also believed to have an astringent effect, which can help to stop bleeding quickly. This herb is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat menstrual problems, and its effectiveness is attributed to its ability to balance the body's energy and restore its natural rhythm.

Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, is a type of plant that has been used for centuries to help with heavy bleeding during periods. This is because it contains compounds called isoflavones, which are similar to estrogen in the body. Estrogen helps to balance the hormones that regulate menstruation, and when taken as a supplement, it can help to reduce heavy bleeding. Red clover isoflavones also have antispasmodic properties, which can help to ease cramps and discomfort that often come with heavy bleeding. Additionally, the plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the abdomen and lower back. Some studies have shown that taking red clover supplements can reduce menstrual bleeding by up to 50%, making it a popular natural remedy for women who experience heavy periods.

Ginkgo biloba is a plant that may help with heavy bleeding during periods. It is thought to work by helping blood vessels to relax and reduce bleeding. The plant contains special chemicals that help to thin the blood and improve blood flow. When these chemicals are absorbed, they may help to reduce the amount of blood lost during a period. This can be especially helpful for women who experience heavy or prolonged bleeding. Some women also use ginkgo biloba to help with other symptoms of PMS, such as mood swings and breast tenderness. Ginkgo biloba is often taken in supplement form, and it's recommended to talk to a doctor before using it as a treatment. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and some studies have shown that it may be effective in reducing menstrual bleeding.

Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is a natural remedy that can help with heavy bleeding during periods. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the uterus, reducing the risk of excessive bleeding. Glycyrrhiza glabra also contains antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the uterine muscles and prevent contractions that can lead to heavy bleeding. Additionally, it has been found to have a balancing effect on hormones, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the risk of heavy bleeding. The root also contains compounds that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate heavy bleeding. Furthermore, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been traditionally used to treat various menstrual disorders, including dysmenorrhea and metrorrhagia, making it a potential natural remedy for women experiencing heavy bleeding during periods.

Ligusticum wallichii is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly to help with menstrual issues. It is believed to stop excessive bleeding during periods due to its ability to reduce inflammation and slow down blood flow. The plant contains compounds that help to contract the blood vessels, which in turn reduces the amount of blood lost during heavy periods. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the uterine lining and reduce cramping, which can help to control excessive bleeding. Additionally, Ligusticum wallichii is thought to help regulate hormonal imbalances that can cause irregular or heavy periods. It is often used in combination with other herbs to treat menstrual issues and is considered a natural remedy for those experiencing over bleeding during their periods.

Angelica archangelica, also known as European angelica or wild celery, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding. The roots and leaves of this plant contain compounds that act as a natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to ease cramps and reduce bleeding. The plant's properties can also help to tone the uterus and reduce the risk of heavy bleeding by regulating the menstrual cycle. Additionally, Angelica archangelica has been shown to have antioxidant and astringent properties, which can help to prevent blood loss by stopping the bleeding and promoting clotting. This plant's natural ability to balance the body's hormone levels can also help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the frequency and severity of heavy bleeding.

preparations.preparation_desc: Herbal decoction is a natural remedy that can help with heavy bleeding during periods. It's made by boiling herbs in water to create a strong tea that helps to balance the body's hormonal system. The herbs used in decoction, such as ginger, turmeric, and parsley, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce menstrual cramps and ease the flow of blood. Some herbs, like dandelion and red clover, are rich in iron and other essential nutrients that can help to build up the body's iron stores and reduce the risk of anemia. By helping to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce inflammation, herbal decoction can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with heavy bleeding during periods. Regular use of herbal decoction can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of heavy bleeding over time, making it a popular natural remedy for women who experience this issue.

Herbal teas can help with heavy bleeding during periods. One reason is that some teas have a compound called tannins, which can help reduce bleeding by stopping the flow of blood vessels. Red clover tea, for example, is high in isoflavones that can help relax the uterus and stop bleeding. Ginger tea is another option, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and cramping. Peppermint tea can also help soothe the stomach and ease nausea, which can be symptoms of heavy bleeding. Chamomile tea, on the other hand, has a calming effect that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can sometimes contribute to heavy bleeding.  Herbal teas can also help regulate the menstrual cycle by balancing hormones, which can lead to lighter and more manageable bleeding. Some teas, like dandelion root tea, have diuretic properties that can help reduce water retention and ease bloating, which can be uncomfortable during periods. Additionally, herbal teas like raspberry leaf tea contain magnesium and other minerals that can help relax the muscles and reduce cramping. These teas work in different ways to help manage heavy bleeding during periods, and they can be a natural and non-invasive way to find relief.

Herbal tinctures can help with heavy bleeding during periods because they contain natural substances that can help regulate hormones and ease cramps. Some herbal tinctures, like those made from ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling in the uterus, which can help control heavy bleeding. Other herbs like saw palmetto and red clover may help balance estrogen levels, which can also contribute to heavy bleeding.  Some herbal tinctures, such as those made from cramp bark and black cohosh, can help relax the uterus and reduce contractions, which can help slow down heavy bleeding. Additionally, certain herbs like dandelion root and yarrow may help stimulate the body to produce more blood clotting factors, which can help stop heavy bleeding. These herbal tinctures can be taken internally in small amounts, usually 20-30 drops per day, mixed with water or another liquid, and can be used in combination with other natural remedies to help manage heavy bleeding.

Herbal infusion is a natural and gentle way to help with heavy bleeding during periods. Some herbs like ginger and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce menstrual cramps and ease heavy bleeding. The antispasmodic properties of herbs like cramp bark and wild yam can also help relax the muscles in the uterus, reducing the frequency and severity of heavy bleeding. Additionally, herbs like red clover and raspberry leaf are rich in iron and can help replenish the body's iron stores, which can become depleted during heavy menstrual bleeding. The soothing and calming effects of herbal infusion can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can sometimes exacerbate heavy bleeding. Drinking herbal infusion regularly can also help regulate menstrual cycles and promote overall balance in the body.

Herbal cream for period bleeding is a natural remedy that can help stop excessive bleeding. Many women suffer from heavy periods, which can be painful and uncomfortable. This cream is made from a combination of herbs like chamomile, calendula, and witch hazel, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These herbs help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and promote clotting, which can stop the bleeding. The cream also cools and soothes the skin, reducing pain and discomfort. When applied to the lower abdomen, it can help to ease cramps and reduce bleeding. Some herbal creams also contain iron-rich herbs like dandelion and plantain, which can help to replenish iron levels lost during heavy bleeding. By applying the cream to the affected area, women can find relief from heavy periods and return to their normal activities.

Herbal gel is a great relief for women who experience heavy bleeding during their periods. This gel is made from natural ingredients that help to reduce blood loss and ease discomfort. It works by reducing inflammation and slowing down blood flow, which helps to stop excessive bleeding. Some herbal gels also contain antiseptic properties that help to prevent infections, which is common during periods. When applied to the lower abdomen, it helps to soothe cramps and reduce pain. The natural ingredients in herbal gel are gentle on the skin and do not have any harsh side effects. It is also easy to apply and can be used throughout the day to provide continuous relief.
